description: irregexp bytecode compiler emitted an assert when the cp_offset (character position offset) for a quantified loop went below kMinCPOffset (the minimum allowed negative lookback). For specific regex shapes the optimizer would compute a cp_offset more negative than supported by the macro-assembler API, hitting the `(kMinCPOffset) <= (cp_offset)` assert. Fix: upstream irregexp patch (git 866e7aa6a080 r=dminor) — extends the compiler to bail-out / clamp at the limit, plus a Mozilla-side macro-assembler tweak. Multi-file: regexp-compiler.cc, regexp-compiler.h, regexp-macro-assembler.cc.
